pattern: DFS from every tree node tries to match the linked list downward; a recursive match helper that also resets to the list head on failure at each tree node keeps the logic clean.
hints: Try starting the linked-list match from every node in the tree; you need a helper that checks whether the list matches a downward path starting at a given tree node.
The match helper recurses: if the current list node is null the entire list matched; if the tree node is null or values differ, fail; otherwise recurse on both children with the next list node.
Combine: DFS across all tree nodes, and at each call the match helper from the root of the list.

edge_inputs.description: list [4,2,8] found as a downward path
list [1,4,2,6] found along a longer path
single-node list not in single-node tree
prefix of the list matches but the full list does not continue
list longer than any root-to-leaf path

stress.description: random small tree with a list that is sometimes present
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    # Build a random tree as BFS array, then plant a path from a random node
    n = max(3, size)
    tree = [rng.randint(1, 10) for _ in range(n)]
    # Add nulls to make it a proper BFS array (randomly null out right children)
    bfs = []
    for i, v in enumerate(tree):
        bfs.append(v)
        if rng.random() < 0.2:
            bfs.append(None)
    # pick a short downward path from the root
    path_len = rng.randint(1, 3)
    path = [bfs[0]]
    idx = 0
    for _ in range(path_len - 1):
        left_idx = 2 * idx + 1
        right_idx = 2 * idx + 2
        children = [c for c in [left_idx, right_idx] if c < len(bfs) and bfs[c] is not None]
        if not children:
            break
        idx = rng.choice(children)
        path.append(bfs[idx])
    return [path, bfs]
